summary: Mobile-first MVP for VibeLoc (NeighbourSwipe), a swipe-based neighbourhood discovery and matching app for urban renters in Kenya (initially Nairobi). Users swipe on neighbourhoods (Tinder-like) to find matches based on lifestyle, commute, and budget before viewing specific houses.
architectural_notes: Mobile-first MVP; swipe-based UX similar to Tinder but for neighbourhoods, not people.
Target users: urban renters / gig-economy workers in Kenya (initial focus: Nairobi).
Core matching factors: lifestyle, commute, and budget; discover neighbourhoods before specific house listings.
imageRequirements:
## Required Images
- App logo mark + wordmark in a modern, warm, Nairobi-inspired style (vibeloc-logo.dim_512x512.png)
- Neighbourhood card placeholder illustration with subtle city/urban motif, warm tones (neighbourhood-placeholder.dim_1080x1350.png)
- Onboarding background pattern/illustration with abstract urban shapes, low-contrast (onboarding-background.dim_1080x1920.png)

existing_features.title: Create a mobile-first React UI shell for the app named “VibeLoc” with the product label “NeighbourSwipe”, including top-level navigation and routing for: Onboarding, Discover (swipe feed), Shortlist (liked neighbourhoods), and Profile/Preferences.

Implement onboarding to capture renter preferences used for matching: monthly budget range (KES), commute preferences (primary area/direction + max commute time), and lifestyle preferences (selectable tags). Persist onboarding completion state and the captured preferences so the user can return later and edit them.

Build the Discover swipe experience similar to Tinder: show one neighbourhood card at a time with image, name, key highlights (rent band, commute note, top tags), and clear swipe actions (Like / Nope) supporting both gestures and buttons.
Persist swipe decisions (like/dislike) per user and implement a “Shortlist” screen that lists liked neighbourhoods with basic details and the ability to remove (unlike) an item.

Add Internet Identity sign-in support so that preferences and likes can be stored per Principal on the backend; provide a clear signed-in/signed-out state in the UI without editing immutable auth hook files.

Define and apply a coherent visual theme suitable for an urban Kenya/Nairobi renter audience (mobile-first), including color palette, typography, and component styling, and use it consistently across all screens.

Integrate generated static images as frontend assets and use them in the UI (logo in header/onboarding; neighbourhood placeholder imagery in cards where applicable), served directly from the frontend (not via backend).

new_feature_requests.summary: Mobile-first onboarding to capture renter preferences (budget, commute, lifestyle)
Neighbourhood discovery feed with swipe interactions (like/dislike) on neighbourhood cards
Matching/recommendation logic to rank neighbourhoods based on user preferences
Saved/liked neighbourhoods list (shortlist) with basic details for revisit

Create a backend neighbourhood catalog for Nairobi, Kenya with an initial curated set of neighbourhood entries (seed data) including: name, short description, typical rent band (KES), commute notes, lifestyle tags, and 1 image reference per neighbourhood (static asset filename).

Implement matching/recommendation logic that ranks neighbourhoods for a user based on their saved preferences (budget fit, commute fit, lifestyle tag overlap). Return a scored, ordered list for the Discover feed.
